seoTitle: Sunnyvale vs Surrey Downs — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Sunnyvale and Surrey Downs. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Sunnyvale vs Surrey Downs: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Sunnyvale and Surrey Downs.
heading: Sunnyvale vs Surrey Downs — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sunnyvale, South Australia and Surrey Downs, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Surrey Downs has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Sunnyvale (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sunnyvale or Surrey Downs safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sunnyvale has a flood risk score of 37/100, while Surrey Downs scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sunnyvale scores 61/100 for bushfire risk. Surrey Downs scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sunnyvale or Surrey Downs?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Surrey Downs has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
